content: Clinical trial failure rates represent the proportion of investigational drugs that do not advance from one development phase to the next or fail to gain regulatory approval. Overall, only about 10-15% of drugs entering Phase I ultimately receive FDA approval. Failure rates vary significantly by therapeutic area: oncology has the highest attrition (~90% fail in Phase II/III), while vaccines and hematology have lower rates. The likelihood of success (LOS) from Phase I to approval averages 7.9% across all indications. Most failures occur in Phase II due to lack of efficacy (40-50% of failures) or safety concerns (20-30%). Understanding failure patterns informs go/no-go decisions and portfolio management.
